# Derivation

Equation-level derivation for **2607.02157**, generated from the reproduction's equation cards (15 equations). Each block is an equation the reproduction depends on, transcribed from the paper with its source location and tagged by derivation status. For the narrative walk-through see `DERIVATION_TRACE.md` (or `METHOD_TRACE.md`).

## Equations
### EQC001 â€” Discrete thermalizing collisional map

*Simulation engine: CPTP evolution of the driven reservoir between injections; thermodynamically consistent (Gibbs state invariant since [U, rho_eq] = 0).*

$$
\rho_{t_{n+1}} = \mathcal{E}_{t_n}(\rho_{t_n}) = (1-P_{\mathrm{th}})\,U_{t_n}\rho_{t_n}U_{t_n}^{\dagger} + P_{\mathrm{th}}\,\rho^{\mathrm{eq}}_{s_n},\quad P_{\mathrm{th}} = 1-e^{-\gamma_0\delta t},\ U_{t_n}=e^{-iH_{t_n}\delta t},\ \rho^{\mathrm{eq}}_{s_n}=\frac{e^{-\beta H_{t_n}}}{\mathrm{Tr}\,e^{-\beta H_{t_n}}}
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `Davies-Lindblad global master equation (SI I), repeated-interaction collision model` Â· source: equation: Eq. (19) / SI Eq. (S7)

Numerical form:

```
Per step: eigendecompose H (2^L x 2^L Hermitian), build U and rho_eq from the same eigenbasis, apply convex combination.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::collisional_step`


### EQC002 â€” Mackey-Glass drive generation and preprocessing

*Chaotic classical input; RK4 integration, transient discarded, sampled every 3 time units, linearly rescaled to [-1,1]; approx zero mean, sigma_s^2 ~= 0.11, spectral peak omega_s ~= 0.36.*

$$
\frac{dx}{dt} = \frac{\beta_{\mathrm{MG}}\,x(t-\tau_{\mathrm{MG}})}{1+x^{10}(t-\tau_{\mathrm{MG}})} - \gamma_{\mathrm{MG}}\,x(t),\quad (\beta_{\mathrm{MG}},\gamma_{\mathrm{MG}},\tau_{\mathrm{MG}}) = (0.2, 0.1, 18)
$$

status: `source_only` Â· source: equation: Eq. (18) + Methods 'Chaotic time-series generation'

Numerical form:

```
RK4 with dt_int = 0.1 and delay buffer; sequences centered and linearly scaled to the paper's stated ensemble variance sigma_s^2 = 0.11 (support within [-1,1]).
```

Code: `code/scripts/mackey_glass.py::generate_mg_sequences`


### EQC003 â€” Holevo memory capacity and relative-entropy recast

*Historical information dynamically retained about the current drive s_n; estimated by binning states by s_n (Methods Eqs. 20-21, B=50).*

$$
\chi^{\mathrm{m}}_{t_{n+1}} = S(\bar\rho_{t_{n+1}}) - \sum_{s_n} P(s_n) S(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_n}) = \sum_{s_n} P(s_n)\, S(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_n} \| \bar\rho_{t_{n+1}})
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `Holevo quantity; linearity of trace (S13)` Â· source: equation: Eq. (8) / SI Eq. (S13)

Numerical form:

```
chi_m = S(rho_bar) - sum_b P_b S(rho_b) with eigenvalue truncation 1e-12; rho_b = bin-averaged density matrix keyed by s_n.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::holevo_capacity`


### EQC004 â€” Holevo predictive capacity

*Statistical correlation between the reservoir state and the upcoming target s_{n+1}; same estimator with bins keyed by s_{n+1}. Marginalization consistency (Eq. 7/S8-S9) guarantees the same unconditional average.*

$$
\chi^{\mathrm{p}}_{t_{n+1}} = S(\bar\rho_{t_{n+1}}) - \sum_{s_{n+1}} P(s_{n+1}) S(\rho^{\mathrm{p}}_{s_{n+1}})
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `EQC003` Â· source: equation: Eq. (9) / SI Eq. (S21)

Numerical form:

```
Identical to EQC003 with bin key s_{n+1}.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::holevo_capacity`


### EQC005 â€” Quantum informational dissipation (QID)

*Non-predictive historical information structurally retained by the reservoir; definition.*

$$
\chi^{\mathrm{d}}_{t_{n+1}} = \chi^{\mathrm{m}}_{t_{n+1}} - \chi^{\mathrm{p}}_{t_{n+1}}
$$

status: `source_only` Â· derived from: `EQC003`, `EQC004` Â· source: equation: Eq. (10)

Numerical form:

```
Difference of the two estimated capacities per step; accumulated over N_eval steps for chi_d_tot.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::step_metrics`


### EQC006 â€” Coherence decomposition of the Holevo quantity

*Splits capacities into classical mutual information (diagonal populations) and ensemble coherence (off-diagonal interference); dephasing basis = sigma^z product basis in which H1 is diagonal.*

$$
\chi = \mathcal{I} + \mathcal{C},\quad \mathcal{I} = S[\Delta(\bar\rho)] - \sum_x P(x) S[\Delta(\rho_x)],\quad \mathcal{C} = \sum_x P(x) C(\rho_x) - C(\bar\rho),\quad C(\rho) = S[\Delta(\rho)] - S(\rho)
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `relative entropy of coherence; linearity of the dephasing map (S54-S55)` Â· source: equation: Eq. (11) / SI Eqs. (S53-S55)

Numerical form:

```
Delta(rho) = diagonal part in computational basis; I and C from binned ensembles.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::coherence_decomposition`


### EQC007 â€” QID split into classical and coherent dissipation

*Splits QID into population dissipation and coherent dissipation; the shared baseline C(rho_bar) cancels because both ensembles average to the same state.*

$$
\chi^{\mathrm{d}} = \mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{c}} + \mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{q}},\quad \mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{c}} = \mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{m}} - \mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{p}},\quad \mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{q}} = \sum_{s_n} P(s_n) C(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_n}) - \sum_{s_{n+1}} P(s_{n+1}) C(\rho^{\mathrm{p}}_{s_{n+1}})
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `EQC005`, `EQC006` Â· source: equation: Eq. (12) / SI Eq. (S56)

Numerical form:

```
Assembled from the same binned conditional states as EQC006.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::coherence_decomposition`


### EQC008 â€” Macroscopic injection work from conditional ensembles

*Average quench work of the signal injection, expressed through the same conditional states used for the capacities; enables macroscopic evaluation without trajectory unraveling.*

$$
W_{n+1} = \sum_{s_{n+1}} P(s_{n+1})\,\mathrm{Tr}(\rho^{\mathrm{p}}_{s_{n+1}} H_{t_{n+1}}) - \sum_{s_n} P(s_n)\,\mathrm{Tr}(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_n} H_{t_n})
$$

status: `source_only` Â· derived from: `microscopic quench work averaged over trajectories, conditioned via the probability product rule` Â· source: equation: SI Eq. (S62)

Numerical form:

```
Energy expectations of binned states against the instantaneous Hamiltonians; H_{t_{n+1}} uses the bin's own s_{n+1} value.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::injection_work`


### EQC009 â€” Per-step identity: irreversible injection work equals QID

*Central identity: energetic terms cancel exactly between W and Delta F, leaving a purely entropic penalty equal to the QID. Machine-checkable in simulation because both sides are algebra on the same binned states.*

$$
\beta W^{\mathrm{irr}}_{n+1} = \beta(W_{n+1} - \Delta\mathcal{F}_{n+1}) = \sum_{s_{n+1}} P S(\rho^{\mathrm{p}}) - \sum_{s_n} P S(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}) = \chi^{\mathrm{m}}_{t_{n+1}} - \chi^{\mathrm{p}}_{t_{n+1}} = \chi^{\mathrm{d}}_{t_{n+1}}
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `EQC003`, `EQC004`, `EQC008`, `non-equilibrium free energy F(rho) = Tr(rho H) - S(rho)/beta` Â· source: equation: Eq. (13) / SI Eqs. (S63-S64)

Numerical form:

```
Checked each simulation step as a consistency invariant.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::step_metrics`


### EQC010 â€” Generalized Landauer bound and non-negative relaxation dissipation

*Fig. 2b content: cumulative irreversible work upper-bounds accumulated QID; relaxation term non-negative by monotonicity of relative entropy under the CPTP map with invariant Gibbs state.*

$$
\beta W^{\mathrm{relax}}_{n+1} = \sum_{s_{n+1}} P\left[S(\rho^{\mathrm{p}}\|\rho^{\mathrm{eq}}) - S(\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_{n+1}}\|\rho^{\mathrm{eq}})\right] \ge 0 \;\Rightarrow\; \beta W^{\mathrm{irr}}_{\mathrm{tot}} \ge \chi^{\mathrm{d}}_{\mathrm{tot}},\quad \beta Q_{\mathrm{diss}} \ge \Delta S_{\mathrm{sys}} + \chi^{\mathrm{d}}_{\mathrm{tot}}
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `EQC001 (Gibbs invariance)`, `EQC009`, `data-processing inequality for quantum relative entropy` Â· source: equation: Eqs. (14)-(15) / SI Eqs. (S67-S71)

Numerical form:

```
W_relax accumulated from relative entropies of binned states vs instantaneous Gibbs states; W_irr_tot = sum(chi_d + beta*W_relax).
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::relaxation_work`


### EQC011 â€” BKM geometric expansion and analytic capacities

*Analytical linear-response capacity used to explain the critical peak (Eq. 17); g_jk = (1-P_th)exp(-i Delta E_jk dt), F_jk = |1-g_jk|^2.*

$$
S(\bar\rho + \lambda\delta\rho\,\|\,\bar\rho) \approx \frac{\lambda^2}{2} g^{\bar\rho}_{\mathrm{BKM}}(\delta\rho,\delta\rho),\quad g_{\mathrm{BKM}} = \sum_{j,k} |(\delta\rho)_{jk}|^2 \frac{\ln p_j - \ln p_k}{p_j - p_k};\quad \chi^{\mathrm{m}} \approx \frac{\lambda^2}{2}\Big[\beta\sum_{j\ne k} G(\Delta E_{jk}) \frac{p_k - p_j}{E_j - E_k} F_{jk} |\langle j|H_1|k\rangle|^2 + \beta^2 P_{\mathrm{th}}^2 \sum_j G(0)\, p_j |\langle j|H_1 - \langle H_1\rangle_0|j\rangle|^2\Big]
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `second-order relative-entropy expansion (S10-S12)`, `partial-fraction integral S39`, `history unrolling S30-S34` Â· source: equation: Eq. (17) / SI Eqs. (S10-S12, S38-S42)

Numerical form:

```
Evaluated in the H0 eigenbasis with thermal populations; G estimated from the drive statistics.
```

Code: `code/scripts/analytics.py::bkm_capacity`


### EQC012 â€” Dynamic accumulation factor and spectral resonance peak

*Frequency-selective filter of the input sequence; resonance Delta E_jk = omega_s explains the critical computational peak (Fig. S1a). With paper values (P_th ~= 0.095, gamma_s dt ~= 0.015, sigma_s^2 ~= 0.11): G(omega_s) ~= 2.3.*

$$
G(\Delta E_{jk}) \approx \frac{1}{\sigma_s^2}\Big|\sum_{\tau=0}^{\infty} C_a(\tau) g_{jk}^{\tau}\Big|^2,\quad C_a(\tau) \approx \sigma_s^2 \cos(\omega_s\tau\delta t) e^{-\gamma_s\tau\delta t},\quad G(\omega_s) \approx \frac{\sigma_s^2}{4(P_{\mathrm{th}} + \gamma_s\delta t)^2}
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `EQC011`, `linear projection estimator S45-S47`, `Wiener-Khinchin Lorentzian model S50` Â· source: equation: SI Eqs. (S43, S49, S52)

Numerical form:

```
G(omega) evaluated from the Lorentzian C_a and directly from binned sequence statistics (S43) for Fig. S1a.
```

Code: `code/scripts/analytics.py::accumulation_factor`


### EQC013 â€” Reservoir architectures: disordered TFIM and augmented cluster model

*The two reservoir Hamiltonians; TFIM has an ergodic-to-MBL crossover in J, the cluster model a symmetry-protected topological transition at alpha ~= 0.5. Boundary condition: open chain, fixed by the PBC duality symmetry argument and the Fig. S1c spectral fingerprint.*

$$
H_0^{\mathrm{TFIM}} = \sum_{i>j} J_{ij}\sigma^z_i\sigma^z_j + h\sum_i \sigma^x_i,\ h=1,\ J_{ij}\sim U[-J/2,J/2];\quad H_0^{\mathrm{cluster}} = -J_{zz}\sum_i \sigma^z_i\sigma^z_{i+1} - h_x\sum_i \sigma^x_i + J_{zxz}\sum_i \sigma^z_{i-1}\sigma^x_i\sigma^z_{i+1},\ J_{zz}=0.1,\ J_{zxz}=(1-J_{zz})\alpha,\ h_x=(1-J_{zz})(1-\alpha)
$$

status: `source_only` Â· source: text: Main text, 'Quantum critical thermodynamics' (p. 7)

Numerical form:

```
Dense 2^L x 2^L Pauli-string construction; cluster chain OPEN (paper convention, resolved empirically); TFIM fully connected.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::tfim_hamiltonian`, `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::cluster_hamiltonian`


### EQC014 â€” Ridge readout and NMSE

*Macroscopic task benchmark (green dashed curves in Fig. 2a and Fig. S2c); eta = 1e-5; features = full Pauli-basis expectation values.*

$$
\mathbf{w}_{\mathrm{opt}} = (\mathbf{X}^T\mathbf{X} + \eta I)^{-1}\mathbf{X}^T\hat{\mathbf{y}},\quad \mathrm{NMSE} = \frac{\|\mathbf{y} - \hat{\mathbf{y}}\|^2}{\|\hat{\mathbf{y}}\|^2}
$$

status: `source_only` Â· source: equation: Eqs. (2)-(3)

Numerical form:

```
Standard ridge regression on training-window features; prediction target s_{n+h}.
```

Code: `code/scripts/qrc_engine.py::nmse_readout`


### EQC015 â€” Markovian bound on coherent dissipation

*Negative coherent dissipation is only possible for non-Markovian drives (like Mackey-Glass); underlies the quantum-advantage claim of Fig. 2c.*

$$
\text{first-order Markov drive} \Rightarrow \rho^{\mathrm{p}}_{s_{n+1}} = \sum_{s_n} P(s_n|s_{n+1})\rho^{\mathrm{m}}_{s_n} \Rightarrow \mathcal{D}^{\mathrm{q}} \ge 0
$$

status: `verified` Â· derived from: `convexity of the relative entropy of coherence applied to the causal mixture S57` Â· source: equation: SI Eqs. (S57-S61)

Numerical form:

```
Not used directly in code; interpretive bound for the sign of D_q.
```
